title: Israel Approves $6B+ Fighter Jet Purchase Including F-35I and F-15IA Squadrons
summary: Israel approved the purchase of approximately 50 advanced fighter jets from the United States, including one F-35I Adir stealth squadron and one F-15IA strike squadron, to expand air superiority capabilities amid the Iran war.
contents:
## Israel Expands Combat Air Fleet

### Procurement Details
Israel approved the purchase of approximately 50 advanced fighter jets from the United States, comprising one full squadron of F-35I Adir multi-role stealth fighters (produced by Lockheed Martin) and one squadron of F-15IA strike fighters (from Boeing). Defense Minister Israel Katz stated: "Our mission remains clear — to ensure the IDF possesses the tools, capabilities and strength to operate anywhere, at any time."

### Combat-Proven Platforms
Israel has used its existing F-35I Adir stealth fighters extensively in both the Gaza and Iran wars. These aircraft have served as one of the Israeli Air Force's most valuable assets for striking Iranian targets, including suppressing air defenses and identifying deep targets.

### Strategic Context
The procurement comes amid the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran and aims to bolster Israel's long-range strike and air superiority capabilities. The F-35I fleet expansion strengthens Israel's ability to conduct operations against hardened targets and through contested airspace.

### Regional Air Power Balance
The deal significantly expands Israel's advanced combat aircraft fleet at a time when regional air power dynamics remain in flux due to the Iran war, Hezbollah operations in Lebanon, and broader Middle East instability.

**Sources:** DW News
